seoTitle: How to Reprint Paychecks in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to reprint paychecks in QuickBooks Online when a check is lost, damaged, or needs a corrected copy for an employee.
intro: Lost or damaged paychecks happen, and reprinting them in QuickBooks Online is a straightforward fix once you know where to look. This guide walks through finding the right paycheck and reprinting it accurately.

steps.heading: Locate the paycheck
steps.body: Go to your payroll history and find the specific paycheck that needs to be reprinted.

steps.heading: Select the reprint option
steps.body: Choose the print or reprint action tied to that paycheck rather than creating a new payroll run.

steps.heading: Confirm the check details
steps.body: Review the pay period, amount, and employee details before printing to make sure everything is accurate.

steps.heading: Print the check
steps.body: Load your check stock and print, double-checking alignment before printing the full batch if needed.

faq.question: Does reprinting a paycheck create a new payroll transaction?
faq.answer: No, reprinting uses the original payroll record rather than generating a new pay run.

faq.question: Can I reprint a paycheck from a previous pay period?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as the paycheck record still exists in your payroll history, it can typically be reprinted.

faq.question: What if the check number needs to change on the reprint?
faq.answer: You can often update the check number when reprinting to reflect the new physical check being issued.

faq.question: Will reprinting affect the employee's pay stub records?
faq.answer: No, reprinting a check doesn't change the underlying pay stub or payroll amounts already recorded.
